speaker: YORK
text: The army of the Queen hath got the field.

speaker: YORK
text: My uncles both are slain in rescuing me;

speaker: YORK
text: And all my followers to the eager foe

speaker: YORK
text: Turn back and fly like ships before the wind,

speaker: YORK
text: Or lambs pursued by hunger-starvèd wolves.

speaker: YORK
text: My sons, God knows what hath bechancèd them;

speaker: YORK
text: But this I know: they have demeaned themselves

speaker: YORK
text: Like men borne to renown by life or death.

speaker: YORK
text: Three times did Richard make a lane to me

speaker: YORK
text: And thrice cried “Courage, father, fight it out!”

speaker: YORK
text: And full as oft came Edward to my side,

speaker: YORK
text: With purple falchion painted to the hilt

speaker: YORK
text: In blood of those that had encountered him;

speaker: YORK
text: And when the hardiest warriors did retire,

speaker: YORK
text: Richard cried “Charge, and give no foot of ground!”

speaker: YORK
text: And cried “A crown or else a glorious tomb;

speaker: YORK
text: A scepter or an earthly sepulcher!”

speaker: YORK
text: With this we charged again; but, out alas,

speaker: YORK
text: We budged again, as I have seen a swan

speaker: YORK
text: With bootless labor swim against the tide

speaker: YORK
text: And spend her strength with over-matching waves.

speaker: YORK
text: Ah, hark, the fatal followers do pursue,

speaker: YORK
text: And I am faint and cannot fly their fury;

speaker: YORK
text: And were I strong, I would not shun their fury.

speaker: YORK
text: The sands are numbered that makes up my life.

speaker: YORK
text: Here must I stay, and here my life must end.

speaker: YORK
text: Come, bloody Clifford, rough Northumberland,

speaker: YORK
text: I dare your quenchless fury to more rage.

speaker: YORK
text: I am your butt, and I abide your shot.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Yield to our mercy, proud Plantagenet.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Ay, to such mercy as his ruthless arm

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: With downright payment showed unto my father.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Now Phaëton hath tumbled from his car

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And made an evening at the noontide prick.

speaker: YORK
text: My ashes, as the Phoenix’, may bring forth

speaker: YORK
text: A bird that will revenge upon you all;

speaker: YORK
text: And in that hope I throw mine eyes to heaven,

speaker: YORK
text: Scorning whate’er you can afflict me with.

speaker: YORK
text: Why come you not? What, multitudes, and fear?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: So cowards fight when they can fly no further;

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: So doves do peck the falcon’s piercing talons;

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: So desperate thieves, all hopeless of their lives,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Breathe out invectives ’gainst the officers.

speaker: YORK
text: O Clifford, but bethink thee once again

speaker: YORK
text: And in thy thought o’errun my former time;

speaker: YORK
text: And, if thou canst for blushing, view this face

speaker: YORK
text: And bite thy tongue that slanders him with cowardice

speaker: YORK
text: Whose frown hath made thee faint and fly ere this.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: I will not bandy with thee word for word,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: But buckler with thee blows twice two for one.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Hold, valiant Clifford, for a thousand causes

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I would prolong a while the traitor’s life.—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Wrath makes him deaf; speak thou, Northumberland.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Hold, Clifford, do not honor him so much

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: To prick thy finger, though to wound his heart.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: What valor were it when a cur doth grin

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: For one to thrust his hand between his teeth,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: When he might spurn him with his foot away?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: It is war’s prize to take all vantages,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And ten to one is no impeach of valor.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Ay, ay, so strives the woodcock with the gin.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: So doth the coney struggle in the net.

speaker: YORK
text: So triumph thieves upon their conquered booty;

speaker: YORK
text: So true men yield with robbers, so o’ermatched.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: What would your Grace have done unto him now?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Brave warriors, Clifford and Northumberland,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Come, make him stand upon this molehill here

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That raught at mountains with outstretchèd arms,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Yet parted but the shadow with his hand.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What, was it you that would be England’s king?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Was ’t you that reveled in our parliament

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And made a preachment of your high descent?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Where are your mess of sons to back you now,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The wanton Edward and the lusty George?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And where’s that valiant crookback prodigy,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Dickie, your boy, that with his grumbling voice

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Was wont to cheer his dad in mutinies?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or, with the rest, where is your darling Rutland?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Look, York, I stained this napkin with the blood

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That valiant Clifford with his rapier’s point

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Made issue from the bosom of the boy;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And if thine eyes can water for his death,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I give thee this to dry thy cheeks withal.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Alas, poor York, but that I hate thee deadly

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I should lament thy miserable state.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I prithee grieve to make me merry, York.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What, hath thy fiery heart so parched thine entrails

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That not a tear can fall for Rutland’s death?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Why art thou patient, man? Thou shouldst be mad;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And I, to make thee mad, do mock thee thus.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Stamp, rave, and fret, that I may sing and dance.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thou would’st be fee’d, I see, to make me sport.—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: York cannot speak unless he wear a crown.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: A crown for York!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And, lords, bow low to him.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Hold you his hands whilst I do set it on.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ay, marry, sir, now looks he like a king.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ay, this is he that took King Henry’s chair,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And this is he was his adopted heir.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But how is it that great Plantagenet

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Is crowned so soon and broke his solemn oath?—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: As I bethink me, you should not be king

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Till our King Henry had shook hands with Death.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And will you pale your head in Henry’s glory

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And rob his temples of the diadem

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Now, in his life, against your holy oath?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: O, ’tis a fault too too unpardonable.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Off with the crown and, with the crown, his head;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And whilst we breathe, take time to do him dead.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: That is my office, for my father’s sake.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Nay, stay, let’s hear the orisons he makes.

speaker: YORK
text: She-wolf of France, but worse than wolves of France,

speaker: YORK
text: Whose tongue more poisons than the adder’s tooth:

speaker: YORK
text: How ill-beseeming is it in thy sex

speaker: YORK
text: To triumph like an Amazonian trull

speaker: YORK
text: Upon their woes whom Fortune captivates.

speaker: YORK
text: But that thy face is vizard-like, unchanging,

speaker: YORK
text: Made impudent with use of evil deeds,

speaker: YORK
text: I would assay, proud queen, to make thee blush.

speaker: YORK
text: To tell thee whence thou cam’st, of whom derived,

speaker: YORK
text: Were shame enough to shame thee, wert thou not shameless.

speaker: YORK
text: Thy father bears the type of King of Naples,

speaker: YORK
text: Of both the Sicils, and Jerusalem,

speaker: YORK
text: Yet not so wealthy as an English yeoman.

speaker: YORK
text: Hath that poor monarch taught thee to insult?

speaker: YORK
text: It needs not, nor it boots thee not, proud queen,

speaker: YORK
text: Unless the adage must be verified

speaker: YORK
text: That beggars mounted run their horse to death.

speaker: YORK
text: ’Tis beauty that doth oft make women proud,

speaker: YORK
text: But God He knows thy share thereof is small.

speaker: YORK
text: ’Tis virtue that doth make them most admired;

speaker: YORK
text: The contrary doth make thee wondered at.

speaker: YORK
text: ’Tis government that makes them seem divine;

speaker: YORK
text: The want thereof makes thee abominable.

speaker: YORK
text: Thou art as opposite to every good

speaker: YORK
text: As the Antipodes are unto us

speaker: YORK
text: Or as the south to the Septentrion.

speaker: YORK
text: O, tiger’s heart wrapped in a woman’s hide,

speaker: YORK
text: How couldst thou drain the lifeblood of the child

speaker: YORK
text: To bid the father wipe his eyes withal,

speaker: YORK
text: And yet be seen to bear a woman’s face?

speaker: YORK
text: Women are soft, mild, pitiful, and flexible;

speaker: YORK
text: Thou, stern, obdurate, flinty, rough, remorseless.

speaker: YORK
text: Bidd’st thou me rage? Why, now thou hast thy wish.

speaker: YORK
text: Wouldst have me weep? Why, now thou hast thy will;

speaker: YORK
text: For raging wind blows up incessant showers,

speaker: YORK
text: And when the rage allays, the rain begins.

speaker: YORK
text: These tears are my sweet Rutland’s obsequies,

speaker: YORK
text: And every drop cries vengeance for his death

speaker: YORK
text: ’Gainst thee, fell Clifford, and thee, false Frenchwoman!

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Beshrew me, but his passions moves me so

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: That hardly can I check my eyes from tears.

speaker: YORK
text: That face of his the hungry cannibals

speaker: YORK
text: Would not have touched, would not have stained with blood;

speaker: YORK
text: But you are more inhuman, more inexorable,

speaker: YORK
text: O, ten times more than tigers of Hyrcania.

speaker: YORK
text: See, ruthless queen, a hapless father’s tears.

speaker: YORK
text: This cloth thou dipped’st in blood of my sweet boy,

speaker: YORK
text: And I with tears do wash the blood away.

speaker: YORK
text: Keep thou the napkin and go boast of this;

speaker: YORK
text: And if thou tell’st the heavy story right,

speaker: YORK
text: Upon my soul, the hearers will shed tears.

speaker: YORK
text: Yea, even my foes will shed fast-falling tears

speaker: YORK
text: And say “Alas, it was a piteous deed.”

speaker: YORK
text: There, take the crown and, with the crown, my curse,

speaker: YORK
text: And in thy need such comfort come to thee

speaker: YORK
text: As now I reap at thy too cruel hand.—

speaker: YORK
text: Hard-hearted Clifford, take me from the world,

speaker: YORK
text: My soul to heaven, my blood upon your heads.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Had he been slaughterman to all my kin,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: I should not for my life but weep with him

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: To see how inly sorrow gripes his soul.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What, weeping ripe, my Lord Northumberland?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Think but upon the wrong he did us all,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And that will quickly dry thy melting tears.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Here’s for my oath; here’s for my father’s death!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And here’s to right our gentle-hearted king.

speaker: YORK
text: Open thy gate of mercy, gracious God.

speaker: YORK
text: My soul flies through these wounds to seek out Thee.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Off with his head, and set it on York gates,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: So York may overlook the town of York.
